summary: Iteration 2 backend-only re-test after httpOnly cookie auth refactor. All 35 pytest tests passed (100%): 27 prior tests (regression) + 8 new tests covering /api/auth/logout, /api/auth/switch/{tid} new {ok,tenant_id} shape, cookie-based auth on /auth/me and /tenant, invalid/expired cookie -> 401 'Invalid token', and explicit Bearer-header backwards-compat guard.

minor.issue: Endpoint is unauthenticated — anyone can hit it; harmless since it just clears a cookie, but consider gating to authenticated users for symmetry.

Cookie response uses secure=True + samesite=lax. Works for cross-site testing because clients send the cookie they receive, but in browsers a same-site frontend on a different sub-path may need samesite=none for strict cross-origin POSTs from third-party widgets. Current preview origin works.
If both cookie and Authorization header are present, cookie wins. Document this precedence to avoid confusion when a stale cookie shadows a fresh Bearer.

critical_code_review_comments: deps.py precedence (cookie over Bearer) is fine but should be documented; stale browser cookies could mask Bearer-based debugging.
auth.py /logout is unauthenticated — acceptable but consider returning the same JSONResponse via the same RedirectResponse-style helper for consistency.
auth.py cookies are set with secure=True + samesite=lax — correct for the HTTPS preview URL; if you ever serve over http locally these won't be stored by the browser. Flag for local dev parity.
switch_tenant: import of JSONResponse is local — fine, but move to module-level imports for consistency.
test_credentials: Seeded TEST_ users/tenants dynamically via core.security.create_jwt + dbmod inserts (TEST_ prefixed). No cleanup performed by design.
seed_data_creation: Same as iteration 1: 3 session-scoped seeded user+tenant+member combos via motor; expired-JWT test uses pyjwt directly with JWT_SECRET from env.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 35 backend tests pass: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/backend_test.py -v. New cookie-auth tests live at the bottom of the file (test_logout_returns_ok_and_clears_cookie, test_switch_tenant_sets_cookie_and_returns_ok, test_switch_tenant_forbidden_for_non_member, test_me_via_cookie_only, test_tenant_endpoint_via_cookie, test_invalid_cookie_returns_401, test_expired_cookie_returns_401, test_bearer_header_still_works). Bearer header is still fully supported as fallback. /switch now returns {ok, tenant_id} with Set-Cookie (httponly) instead of {token}.
rca of the issue: No defects found. All review-request acceptance criteria met.
